data.title: Apellis Pharmaceuticals GAAP EPS of -$0.29 beats by $0.10, revenue of $212.5M beats by $14.59M
data.description:
• Apellis Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:APLS): Q4 GAAP EPS of -$0.29 beats by $0.10.
  • Revenue of $212.5M (+45.2% Y/Y) beats by $14.59M.
  • Total revenue was $212.5 million for the fourth quarter of 2024, which consisted of $167.8 million in U.S. net product revenue of SYFOVRE, $23.4 million in U.S. net product revenue of EMPAVELI and $21.4 million in licensing and other revenue associated with the Sobi collaboration.

data.title: Chemtrade Logistics Income Fund reports Q4 results
data.description:
• Chemtrade Logistics Income Fund press release (OTC:CGIFF): Q4 net earnings of C$10.3M, a decrease of C$1.4M year-over-year.
  • Revenue of C$446.5M (+5.8% Y.Y).
  • The company reiterated 2025 guidance for adjusted EBITDA of between C$430M and C$460M.

data.title: European market dips amid tariff concerns, auto sector faces pressure
LafargeHolcim Non-GAAP EPS of CHF 5.70, revenue of CHF 26.41B; initiates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• LafargeHolcim press release (OTCPK:HCMLF): FY Non-GAAP EPS of CHF 5.70.
  • Revenue of CHF 26.41B (-2.2% Y/Y).
  • Recurring EBIT of CHF 5,049m, +10.8% in local currency, +6.1% in CHF.
  • Free cash flow after leases of CHF 3,801 million, up 2.6%.
  • Proposed 11% increase in dividend to CHF 3.10.

  • Outlook and guidance

    Building on its record full-year performance, Holcim has a strong outlook across all of its business segments as follows:

    • North America: Infrastructure modernization and reshoring of manufacturing to drive growth in 2025
    • Latin America: Public and private sectors to drive infrastructure and commercial investments
    • Europe: Continued strong demand for sustainable building solutions expected to drive profitable growth
    • Asia, Middle East & Africa: Strong domestic demand in North Africa, positive outlook in Australia, price recovery in China
    • Solutions & Products: Favorable outlook for both new construction and repair & refurbishment market

    Well positioned for 2025, Holcim expects continued profitable growth with:

    • Mid-single digit Net Sales growth in local currency
    • Over proportional growth in recurring EBIT
    • Further expansion of recurring EBIT margin
    • Free cash flow of above CHF 3.5 billion
    • Continued double-digit growth in recycled construction demolition materials

data.title: ANI Pharmaceuticals beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; updates FY25 outlook
data.description:
• ANI Pharmaceuticals press release (NASDAQ:ANIP): Q4 Non-GAAP EPS of $1.63 beats by $0.19.
  • Revenue of $190.6M (+44.7% Y/Y) beats by $15.25M.
  • Adjusted non-GAAP EBITDA for the fourth quarter of 2024 was $50.0 million, an increase of 65.7% from the fourth quarter of 2023.
  • As of December 31, 2024, the company had $144.9 million in unrestricted cash and cash equivalents, $221.7 million in net accounts receivable and $639.2 million in principal value of outstanding debt (inclusive of our senior convertible notes).
  • The company generated year-to-date cash flow from operations of $64.0 million.
Revised Full Year 2025 Guidance:

ILUVIEN and YUTIQ Net Revenue $97 million - $103 million n/p $32 million n/m
Adjusted Non-GAAP EBITDA $190 million - $200 million $182 million - $192 million $156 million 22% - 28%

data.title: WeRide participates in autonomous driving service for commercial use in France
data.description:
WeRide (NASDAQ:WRD) announced on Friday the first European fully driverless commercial deployment of its Robobus. The deployment was part of a shuttle service partnership with beti, Renault Group (OTCPK:RNSDF), and Macif that will feature an L4-level automated mobility service in France's Drôme region.

For the automated mobility service, WeRide (NASDAQ:WRD) will provide its L4-level autonomous technology for a passenger transport service on open roads in mixed traffic, with several six-meter-long robobuses, each able to carry eight passengers. Beti Automated Mobility will begin operations on March 10, at the Rovaltain business park – Valence TGV station in Drôme. The route is 3.3 km long and serves the train station, off-site long-term parking, the business park's catering hub, and 150 companies employing 3,000 people.

The initial learning phase for the automated mobility service will run from March 10 to April 19, operating Monday to Friday under real passenger transport conditions.

Shares of WeRide (WRD) were down 0.8% in premarket trading on Friday, after plunging 15.0% on Thursday. The robotaxi pure play stock is still up almost 40% on a year-to-date basis.
